page.title: We need senior contract capacity | Bc. Ondřej Halata (halatao.cz)
page.h1: We need senior contract capacity inside the team
page.description: I help companies and product teams when delivery is slowing down because technical ownership is weak, risky areas lack a senior owner, or the team needs stronger contract support.

hero.title: The team is busy, but the critical technical decisions still have no real owner
hero.subtitle: A common situation when backlog keeps moving, but architecture, risky delivery areas, and takeover work are not being handled with enough seniority.

intro: Not every difficult delivery situation needs a new agency or a larger outsourcing model. Often the real need is stronger senior capacity inside the existing team with ownership of a defined area.
The signal is usually a team that handles day-to-day work but keeps slowing down on architecture, takeover, release confidence, or technically sensitive project phases.

sections.title: Typical symptoms
body: Without stronger senior capacity, the project often keeps moving but with more technical debt, weaker decisions, and an increasingly tired delivery team.
bullets: complex changes keep being delayed or revisited
risky parts of the system have no clear owner
technical decision-making is slow or inconsistent
the project needs takeover, stabilisation, or stronger architecture input

sections.title: How I approach it
body: Instead of generic staff augmentation, I focus on defined ownership: taking over a risky technical area, stabilising a problem workstream, or strengthening the team in a phase where senior judgement matters most.

sections.title: What a good outcome looks like
body: The goal is not a quick patch. The goal is to restore control, confidence, and a practical next step.
bullets: faster progress in the blocked part of the project
less pressure on the internal team in a critical phase
clearer connection between engineering and business decisions

faq.question: Is this suitable for smaller teams too?
faq.answer: Yes, if they need to add experience quickly, take over a risky area, or get through an important delivery phase with more confidence.

faq.question: Does this have to be a long contract?
faq.answer: No. Some engagements are shorter and focused on takeover, stabilisation, or helping a specific phase land well.

faq.question: How do we know another developer is not enough?
faq.answer: When the bottleneck is not volume of work but ownership, architecture, release safety, or making the hard technical calls consistently.

for: product or internal teams in an important delivery phase
situations where a risky area needs to be taken over inside the current team
notFor: commodity staffing with no ownership scope
projects with no decision-maker or internal owner

cta.label: Explore the project fit
cta.note: A short summary of the current state and the main risk is enough to start.
